channel.title: Brazil Elections 2026
channel.description: Real-time monitoring of the 2026 Brazilian presidential elections.

item.title: Haddad lança desafio sobre situação das contas públicas às vésperas de deixar governo
item.description: Finance Minister Fernando Haddad, who is about to leave Lula's government to run in this year's municipal elections, launched a public challenge on his social media to all his predecessors in the role, questioning the management of public accounts. He highlighted the improvement in fiscal indicators during his tenure and provoked former Finance Ministers to compare their performances. The move comes on the eve of his candidacy, positioning him as a defender of fiscal balance.

item.title: Lula telefona para aliados sobre terrorismo, mas ignora escândalos no Rio
item.description: Lula called the presidents of Colombia and Mexico to discuss the U.S. proposal to label Brazilian criminal factions as terrorist organizations, while overlooking local scandals in Rio de Janeiro, such as the arrest of councilman Salvino Oliveira (PSD), former youth secretary under Mayor Eduardo Paes, accused of electoral ties to drug trafficker Edgar Alves de Andrade of the Red Command. Despite Paes being a Lula ally, the president did not contact him regarding the case.

item.title: Após visita, Flávio Bolsonaro diz que o pai está abatido e com sinais de cansaço
item.description: Senator Flávio Bolsonaro reported that former President Jair Bolsonaro is fatigued and showing signs of exhaustion after experiencing hiccup episodes, along with worsening kidney function and elevated inflammatory markers, according to the DFStar Hospital bulletin. Despite the clinical deterioration, Bolsonaro's condition remains stable. He was transferred from the 'Papudinha' military prison to the hospital after developing fever, nausea, and chills.

item.title: Diretora de ‘A Voz de Hind Hajab’ elogia Kleber Mendonça Filho e ‘O Agente Secreto’
item.description: Tunisian director Kaouther Ben Hania, nominated for the 2026 Oscar for 'The Voice of Hind Hajab', praised Brazilian filmmaker Kleber Mendonça Filho's 'The Secret Agent' during a panel with fellow International Feature Film nominees. She expressed deep admiration for the film’s ensemble cast, unforgettable characters, and atmospheric storytelling, saying she felt 'at home' watching it and drawing parallels to Tunisian cinema through shared themes of dictatorship and social climate.

item.title: Novo aciona Erika Hilton no Conselho de Ética por ação contra Ratinho
item.description: The Novo party filed an ethics complaint in the Chamber of Deputies against federal deputy Erika Hilton (Psol-SP) for alleged breach of decorum, claiming she systematically uses her mandate to pursue critics through legal actions over statements on gender and biological sex. The complaint follows Hilton’s successful MP-SP action against TV host Ratinho for transphobia, seeking R$10 million in moral damages to be donated to anti-transphobia efforts. Novo argues her conduct constitutes an abuse of parliamentary privileges and a violation of the Chamber’s Ethics Code.

item.title: Registro da Papudinha mostra zelo com saúde de Bolsonaro
item.description: The medical attendance record of Jair Bolsonaro at Papudinha indicates that his health is being closely monitored, contradicting statements by his son Flávio Bolsonaro, who claimed that 'they are playing with his health.' The PL presidential candidate suggested that closed-regime imprisonment would put Bolsonaro at risk of death, but the medical documents do not support this claim.

item.title: “Assinaria quantas vezes fossem necessárias”, diz Flávio sobre CPI contra ministros
item.description: Senator Flávio Bolsonaro (PL-RJ) stated he would sign the request for a parliamentary inquiry commission (CPI) against STF ministers as many times as necessary, denying a Veja magazine report claiming he regretted his signature. He reaffirmed his support for investigating the justices.

item.title: Pedro Simon diz que cadeia não ficaria mal para Moraes
item.description: Former governor and senator Pedro Simon, aged 96, stated that STF Minister Alexandre de Moraes "would not look bad in jail," describing the current moment as the "saddest page" in Brazil's history. He also criticized the Lula government, claiming its ministries lack capacity and that the country is in a permanent crisis. Simon, who supported Simone Tebet in 2022, lamented the alleged decline in respect for the Supreme Federal Court.

item.title: O plano de reação do Centrão a eventuais condenações no caso Master
item.description: Investigations into the Banco Master scandal revealed that public pension funds, tied to powerful politicians, invested millions in the scheme, involving authorities from all three branches of government. Former banker Daniel Vorcaro had direct contact with STF ministers Alexandre de Moraes and Dias Toffoli, and received support from lawmakers like Ciro Nogueira to benefit the bank. Facing pressure, Toffoli declared himself suspect, and there are efforts to bury the case, with an STF minister downplaying Vorcaro’s messages to Moraes as mere imprudence without criminal consequence.

item.title: Flávio diz que assinaria “quantas vezes fosse preciso” CPI contra ministros
item.description: Senator Flávio Bolsonaro (PL-RJ) stated he would sign as many times as necessary to support a CPI investigating ministers involved in illegality, denying a Veja report claiming he regretted backing the Toga CPI. He said his signature was intended to avoid being accused by Senator Alessandro Vieira of having 'something to hide,' noting that Vieira’s CPI request against STF ministers Alexandre de Moraes and Dias Toffoli in the Banco Master case is illegal and electorally motivated.

item.title: Motta se aproxima do governo Lula e ganha o apelido Huguevara
item.description: Hugo Motta, speaker of the Chamber of Deputados, earned the nickname 'Huguevara' after backing Lula government agendas such as ending the 6x1 work scale and regulating app-based work. His rapprochement with the president follows a period of tension, driven in part by his desire to secure Lula’s support for electing his father, Nabor Wanderley, to the Senate from Paraíba.

item.title: Ronaldo Caiado oficializa filiação ao PSD em disputa para concorrer à Presidência
item.description: Governor of Goiás, Ronaldo Caiado, officially joined the PSD in Jaraguá, competing for the party's presidential nomination alongside Ratinho Junior and Eduardo Leite. The PSD plans to announce its presidential candidate by the end of March, aiming to provide an alternative to Brazil's political polarization. According to a Genial/Quaest poll, Ratinho Jr. leads with 7% voting intention, followed by Caiado (4%) and Leite (3%).

item.title: O Lula das sortes tem poucos meios de lidar com o azar das sujeiras de Trump
item.description: The article analyzes how Donald Trump's trade war is affecting economic expectations in Brazil, particularly forecasts for a decline in the Selic interest rate. Despite initial bets on significant rate cuts by the end of 2026, the global conflict has raised rates, with the market now believing the Central Bank may not cut Selic at all (38% probability) or only make a minimal cut (55% chance for 0.25 percentage point). Uncertainty is worsened by Trump's unpredictability, which complicates Brazilian economic planning.

item.title: PL lança Marcos Rogério ao governo de Rondônia e Máximo para o Senado
item.description: Senator Marcos Rogério (PL-RO) was launched as the Liberal Party's pre-candidate for governor of Rondônia in an event in Ji-Paraná, attended by Flávio Bolsonaro. The party also confirmed Fernando Máximo and Bruno Scheid as its Senate pre-candidates. Other mentioned pre-candidates include Adailton Fúria (PSD) and Hildon Chaves (PSDB) for governor, and Confúcio Moura (MDB) and Sílvia Cristina (PP) for the Senate.

item.title: Eduardo e Renan visitam arena de rodeios no Texas
item.description: Eduardo Bolsonaro and Jair Renan Bolsonaro visited the Cowtown Coliseum rodeo arena in Fort Worth, Texas, during a St. Patrick’s Day-themed event. The brothers are in the U.S. while former President Jair Bolsonaro remains in intensive care at DF Star Hospital in Brasília with bacterial bronchopneumonia, clinically stable but with worsening kidney function. During the visit, Eduardo also met with PL congressmen to discuss regional electoral support strategies.

item.title: É #FAKE que Trump tenha suspendido a entrada de brasileiros após Lula cancelar visto de assessor americano
item.description: It is false that Donald Trump suspended the entry of all Brazilians into the U.S. after Lula canceled the visa of Darren Beattie, a Trump advisor. The circulated image is a fabricated fake headline from O GLOBO with no basis in reality. The U.S. government has not issued any official retaliatory statement regarding Brazil's visa revocation.

item.title: Contrato bem feito de delação de Vorcaro ajudaria na faxina do país
item.description: The possible plea bargain of Daniel Vorcaro, currently in preventive detention, and the potential granting of house arrest to Jair Bolsonaro (PL) are two pressing topics in the current political landscape. Vorcaro's cooperation could aid in uncovering corruption schemes, contributing to a national 'clean-up', while Bolsonaro's potential house arrest raises debate over differential treatment of political figures. Both issues highlight tensions between justice, impunity, and accountability of leaders.

item.title: União das direitas na França ronda eleições municipais, vistas como prévias da presidencial
item.description: The municipal elections in Paris, Lyon, and Marseille in March 2026 are seen as a preview of the 2027 presidential election. Rachida Dati, the traditional right-wing candidate and former Sarkozy culture minister, leads in the polls and may need a controversial alliance with far-right figure Sarah Knafo in the runoff to defeat socialist Emmanuel Grégoire. The normalization of a 'union of the right' between traditional Gaullists and neo-fascist-origin far-right forces, once unthinkable, could foreshadow a similar dynamic in the upcoming presidential race.

item.title: Entrada de Simone Tebet na disputa pelo Senado cria impasse no PSB paulista
item.description: The entry of Minister Simone Tebet (MDB) into the Senate race in São Paulo has created an impasse within the state PSB, which wants both her and Minister Márcio França to run for the two Senate seats, despite França having internal preference. The PT is also considering launching Marina Silva, while government allies see the Senate as a more winnable race given Tarcísio de Freitas’ strength in the state gubernatorial race.

item.title: Michel Temer vê um Brasil disfuncional
item.description: Former president Michel Temer criticizes Brazil's current democratic state, pointing to institutional dysfunction due to the three branches' indifference to the Constitution. He advocates for a leader with a unifying spirit in the presidency but says his age prevents him from returning, suggesting Lula should also acknowledge his limits at 80. Temer argues that personal hatred hinders consensus-building and that the solution lies with a new generation that values coexistence among dissenters as central to democracy.

item.title: O rapper que se prepara para se tornar primeiro-ministro
item.description: Rapper Balendra Shah, known as Balen, emerged as a leader of a movement against corruption, unemployment, and elite privilege in Nepal following protests in September 2025. With the support of 800,000 first-time Gen Z voters, he defeated former Prime Minister KP Sharma Oli and led his party to a landslide victory over the old ruling establishment. A former mayor of Kathmandu, Balen uses AI-produced jingles to speak directly to the youth who led the protests.

item.title: Maioria dos paulistas culpa Sabesp por racionar água, diz Datafolha
item.description: A Datafolha survey conducted in 71 municipalities in São Paulo state found that 53% of respondents blamed Sabesp for the need to ration water, surpassing criticism aimed at the state (17%), municipal (12%), and federal (7%) governments. Combined, blame attributed to the three levels of government (36%) falls short of the responsibility assigned directly to the company. Sabesp stated its measures follow regulator directives and that water savings since August could supply Greater São Paulo for 20 days.

item.title: Tarcísio nacionaliza campanha para ajudar Flávio e atacar Haddad
item.description: São Paulo Governor Tarcísio de Freitas plans to nationalize his 2026 re-election campaign, focusing criticism on President Lula’s government and economic performance to bolster his base and support allies like Flávio Bolsonaro, while directly attacking Finance Minister Fernando Haddad.

item.title: Caiado oficializa filiação ao PSD e tenta viabilizar candidatura ao Planalto
item.description: Goiás Governor Ronaldo Caiado officially joined the Social Democratic Party (PSD) in an event in Jaraguá (GO), after União Brasil ruled out launching a presidential candidate for 2026. Caiado said he accepted the invitation from PSD president Gilberto Kassab to pursue his presidential bid. The move signals his effort to consolidate a national political project after departing from União Brasil.

item.title: PL lança pré-candidatura de Marcos Rogério ao governo de Rondônia
item.description: The Liberal Party (PL) launched the pre-candidacy of Senator Marcos Rogério for governor of Rondônia in the 2026 elections at an event in Ji-Paraná, attended by Flávio Bolsonaro, who is also a pre-candidate for president. Federal Deputy Fernando Máximo joined the party during the event.

item.title: PSD não será acessório de nenhum candidato, diz Caiado
item.description: Ronaldo Caiado, PSD’s presidential pre-candidate, stated the party will launch its own presidential candidate in 2026, rejecting any role as vice or accessory to another ticket. He emphasized that the three PSD pre-candidates — himself, Eduardo Leite (RS), and Ratinho Júnior (PR) — are morally committed to supporting the chosen nominee, and the party will announce its candidate by the end of March.

item.title: "Não conseguia se equilibrar" diz Flávio Bolsonaro sobre o pai
item.description: Senator Flávio Bolsonaro (PL-RJ) stated that former President Jair Bolsonaro appeared weakened, with a soft voice and loss of balance shortly before being hospitalized for bilateral bacterial pneumonia. He said the attending physician, Dr. Leandro, warned that Bolsonaro had narrowly avoided serious complications. Flávio also referenced a prior incident where Supreme Court Minister Alexandre de Moraes took 24 hours to authorize hospitalization.

item.title: Pressa de Cármen surpreende e julgamento de Castro deve ser adiado
item.description: Minister Cármen Lúcia, president of the TSE, surprised by scheduling the trial against Governor Cláudio Castro (PL) for March 24, despite Minister Kassio Nunes Marques still analyzing the case. The push to accelerate the judgment contrasts with her usual caution and occurs less than two months before Castro must resign to run for the Senate, where he is the favorite. TSE ministers believe voters should decide punishment at the polls, as the case dates to 2022 and remains unresolved.

item.title: Prisão de Bolsonaro na Papuda pode ser “fatal”, diz Bia Kicis
item.description: Federal deputy Bia Kicis (PL-DF) argued that former President Jair Bolsonaro should serve his sentence under house arrest, claiming his health condition makes imprisonment in Papuda or elsewhere potentially 'fatal'. She stated Bolsonaro requires constant care, with Michelle Bolsonaro monitoring his sleep. Speaking outside DF Star Hospital where Bolsonaro is hospitalized, Kicis also implied connections between STF ministers who convicted him and banker Daniel Vorcaro, amid controversies involving the Banco Master.

item.title: Novo pede cassação de Erika Hilton por reação a comentário de Ratinho
item.description: The Novo party has requested the cassation of federal deputy Erika Hilton (PSol-SP) for alleged breach of parliamentary decorum, citing her reactions to criticism, including legal actions against TV host Ratinho after he stated the Women's Rights Committee presidency should be held by a biological woman, and her use of offensive language on social media.

item.title: Vilela vencerá eleição para governo de Goiás no 1º turno, diz Caiado
item.description: Governor of Goiás Ronaldo Caiado (PSD) stated that Vice Governor Daniel Vilela (MDB) will win the state gubernatorial election in the first round, during an event in Jaraguá where he officially joined the PSD. Caiado, who will leave office at the end of March to run for President, said his political project will continue through Vilela, who assumes on March 31. He also emphasized that the three PSD presidential pre-candidates (himself, Eduardo Leite, and Ratinho Júnior) have a moral commitment to support whoever is chosen by the party.

item.title: “Vamos resgatar o país das mãos sujas do PT”, diz Flávio em Rondônia
item.description: Senator Flávio Bolsonaro (PL-RJ), a pre-candidate for president, participated in an event in Ji-Paraná (RO) where he stated his group aims to 'rescue the country from the dirty hands of the PT'. During the event, he supported the pre-candidacy of Marcos Rogério for governor of Rondônia and cited affiliations and articulations for Senate and state government slates, emphasizing anti-corruption proposals and hardening of criminal law.

item.title: Três sinais do atual momento político brasileiro
item.description: The text criticizes the PT for denying involvement in scandals while actively obstructing parliamentary investigations (CPMIs), arguing that a clean government has nothing to fear from probes. It further suggests that two conservative candidates—such as Flávio Bolsonaro and Ratinho Jr.—should establish a non-aggression pact to consolidate the conservative vote against Lula, modeled after Chile’s 2021 election, where right-wing candidates united behind the finalist.

item.title: Direita pode ganhar eleição por ter quebrado um banco
item.description: The article argues that the Brazilian right may win the 2026 elections as a reward for having caused the collapse of Banco Master, noting that most federative entities that invested pensioners' funds in the bank were governed by right-wing politicians. Key figures cited include Cláudio Castro (RJ), Ibaneis Rocha (DF), Ciro Nogueira, and Filipe Barros, who backed measures to save the bank despite systemic risks. The evangelical church and Faria Lima’s financial sector are highlighted as right-wing power centers in the scandal.

item.title: Três Poderes: Erika Hilton é a vencedora da semana; STF, o perdedor
item.description: Federal deputy Erika Hilton (PSOL-SP) is named winner of the week for becoming the first transgender person to preside over the Chamber of Deputies' Women's Commission. The Supreme Federal Court (STF) is labeled the week's loser due to record-low public approval tied to its involvement in the Master case. Additional highlights include Daniel Vorcaro's plea bargain negotiations, Fernando Haddad's farewell from the Ministry of Finance, and Simone Tebet's potential move to the PSB to run for Senate.

item.title: Por que 'O Agente Secreto', longe de 'Ainda Estou Aqui', ainda é sucesso de bilheteria
item.description: Kleber Mendonça Filho's 'The Secret Agent' reached 2.4 million viewers in Brazil by March 8, 2026, half the audience of 'Still Here' during the same period in 2025. Despite this, the film significantly exceeds the box office average for recent Brazilian productions and is already the seventh-highest-grossing national film since 2018, reflecting relative success in a challenging cinematic landscape.

item.title: Tarcísio e Haddad antecipam disputa mais dura por SP e miram gestões um do outro
item.description: The race between Tarcísio de Freitas (Republicanos) and Fernando Haddad (PT) for the São Paulo governorship in 2026 is expected to be tougher than their 2022 contest, according to allies of both pre-candidates. Each is preemptively targeting the other's administration, signaling a more intense ideological and performance-based battle ahead.

item.title: “Mais uma vez meu pai escapou por pouco”, diz Flávio
item.description: Senator Flávio Bolsonaro (PL-RJ) stated that former President Jair Bolsonaro 'escaped by a hair's breadth' from death after being diagnosed with bacterial bronchopneumonia and admitted to the ICU at DF Star Hospital in Brasília. According to Flávio, the former president would have faced serious complications had he remained untreated for just one or two more hours.

item.title: Preço do diesel sobe quase 12% na semana e litro chega a R$ 6,80, mostra ANP
item.description: Diesel prices rose 11.9% in the week of March 8–14, reaching R$6.80 per liter, according to ANP, driven by international oil prices and a Petrobras refinery price increase of R$0.38 per liter. The government introduced measures to cushion consumer impact, including zeroing PIS and Cofins taxes on diesel imports and sales, anti-speculation oversight, and a R$0.32 per liter subsidy for producers and importers. Petrobras estimates the refinery hike will translate to only R$0.06 per liter at the pump due to these mitigating policies.

item.title: Economia pode crescer 1% no primeiro trimestre, diz Haddad
item.description: Finance Minister Fernando Haddad stated that Brazil's economy could grow between 0.8% and 1% in the first quarter of 2026, driven by ongoing reforms, including the upcoming tax reform. He also confirmed he will leave the Ministry of Finance next week to run in the 2026 elections, without specifying the office, after abandoning his initial plan to support Lula's reelection due to a more challenging political outlook than expected.

item.title: Alckmin: candidaturas de Haddad e Tebet são muito bem-vindas
item.description: Vice President Geraldo Alckmin welcomed the candidacies of Ministers Fernando Haddad (Finance) and Simone Tebet (Planning) as 'very welcome', but avoided confirming his own potential candidacy for 2026. He made the remarks during a visit to a Scania dealership near Brasília, maintaining caution about his political future.

item.title: O governador cotado para ser ministro da Segurança Pública num eventual governo de Flávio
item.description: Flávio Bolsonaro was advised by influential allies to appoint Goiás Governor Ronaldo Caiado as Minister of Public Security in a potential government, should he defeat Lula in 2026. Caiado, still seen as a potential presidential candidate for the PSD, has not been consulted yet, but bolsonaristas view the move as viable in a scenario of a runoff against Lula.

item.title: Declínio de Ibaneis alça Michelle Bolsonaro a principal articuladora da direita no DF
item.description: The decline of Governor Ibaneis Rocha (MDB) due to the Master case has elevated Michelle Bolsonaro (PL) as the main right-wing articulator in the Federal District. She is backing Celina Leão (PP) for governor, but this is causing tension within the PL, which prefers to field its own candidate or support José Roberto Arruda (PSD), who is currently ineligible. Despite internal discomfort, PL president Valdemar Costa Neto has allowed parliamentarians to support other candidates, while maintaining Michelle as the party's key reference in the DF.

item.title: Congressistas contestam Erika Hilton na Comissão da Mulher
item.description: Lawmakers from PL and the Missão party (linked to MBL) challenged Erika Hilton’s (Psol-SP) election as president of the Women’s Defense Committee, claiming her transgender identity disqualifies her from representing women’s interests. Nikolas Ferreira called for mobilization to obstruct the committee and promoted a digital petition with 248,000 signatures, while Missão filed a cassation request over Hilton’s social media post criticizing transfobics. Hilton’s team says the post was directed at bigots, and the matter will be reviewed by the Chamber’s Ethics Council.

item.title: O otimismo de Jair Bolsonaro com os resultados das pesquisas eleitorais
item.description: Former president Jair Bolsonaro, imprisoned for attempting a coup, expressed optimism to allies about his son, Senator Flávio Bolsonaro (PL-RJ), performing well in electoral polls, showing a tie with Lula at 41% in a potential runoff, up from 32% in August 2023. This nine-point gain contrasts with Lula’s seven-point drop from 48% to 41%. Senator Wellington Fagundes (PL-MT) noted Bolsonaro appeared in good health and cheerful during their March 7 meeting, shortly before his hospitalization for bronchopneumonia.

item.title: “Não quero candidatura gravitando mais próximo” de Flávio ou Lula, diz Leite
item.description: Rio Grande do Sul Governor Eduardo Leite (PSD) said he does not want his potential 2026 presidential candidacy to 'gravitate' between Flávio Dino or Luiz Inácio Lula da Silva, stressing the need for political independence from his fellow party members Ronaldo Caiado and Ratinho Júnior, who are also considered possible candidates.

item.title: A sociedade secreta de Lulinha com o “Careca do INSS”
item.description: Fábio Luis da Silva, known as Lulinha, son of President Lula, was investigated for alleged involvement in an INSS fraud scheme that diverted over R$4 billion from elderly pensions. He reportedly planned a business partnership with lobbyist Antonio Carlos Camilo Antunes, known as the 'Careca do INSS', to produce cannabis-based medicines, including a 2024 trip to Portugal, but the deal collapsed amid the scandal. Despite claims of receiving a monthly R$300,000 allowance, Lulinha denies any payment and asserts the partnership never materialized, while his bank and tax secrecy were partially suspended by the STF due to insufficient justification.

item.title: Assessor de Cláudio Castro: 'Palácio da Guanabara é gabinete do crime organizado'
item.description: Victor Travancas, advisor to the Rio de Janeiro Governor’s Civil House, was dismissed after claiming in a podcast that the Guanabara Palace operates as an 'organized crime cabinet'. He stated he had warned Governor Cláudio Castro about the Ceperj scandal involving state funds used for election operatives, and said he had requested resignation due to disagreement with government corruption.

item.title: Assessor de Cláudio Castro: ‘Palácio da Guanabara é gabinete do crime organizado’
item.description: Victor Travancas, a former advisor in Rio de Janeiro’s government, was dismissed after claiming on a podcast that the Guanabara Palace operates as an office of organized crime. He stated he had previously warned Governor Cláudio Castro about the Ceperj scandal, involving the use of state funds to hire electoral operatives, now under trial at the TSE. The trial, suspended until March 24, currently shows a 2-0 vote in favor of convicting the governor for abuse of political and economic power in the 2022 elections.

item.title: Oito anos depois, a inversão de papéis entre Lula e Bolsonaro
item.description: Eight years after Lula's imprisonment in 2018, former President Jair Bolsonaro, currently detained at the Papudinha complex for attempting a coup, is replicating the electoral strategy Lula used while imprisoned: coordinating the PL campaign, selecting candidates (such as Flávio Bolsonaro for president and Marcos Pollon for Senate in Mato Grosso do Sul), defining alliances, and issuing directives via letters and visits. This role reversal highlights how both leaders used incarceration to maintain political control over their parties and alliances.
